zh.Betuwe, Gelderland, Netherlands: 荷蘭蓋爾德藍德貝圖維
zh.lifeSummary: 當Hendrik II van Cuyck van Malsen於大約1140年出生在荷蘭蓋爾德藍德貝圖維時，他的父親Herman II van Cuijk38歲，母親Alvaradis De Chiny31歲。他於大約1160年與Sophia Rhenen Lady of Herpen結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1204年享年65歲。

zh-hans.Betuwe, Gelderland, Netherlands: 荷兰蓋爾德藍德貝圖維
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hendrik II van Cuyck van Malsen于大约1140年出生在荷兰蓋爾德藍德貝圖維时，他的父亲Herman II van Cuijk38岁，母亲Alvaradis De Chiny31岁。他于大约1160年与Sophia Rhenen Lady of Herpen结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1204年享年65岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Cuyck
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Flemish: nickname for a fat man, from an old spelling of Dutch buik ‘belly’.
Flemish: from a derivative of the ancient Germanic personal name Burghard (see Burkhart ); compare German Buck .
Dutch: variant, mostly archaic or Americanized, of Buijk or Buik, cognates of 1 and 2 above.

variants.name: Burkhart
meanings: German: from the medieval personal name Burkhard, from ancient Germanic Burghard, composed of the elements burg ‘fort, castle’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’. This form of the surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and in Czechia (compare 2 below), but it is very rare there. Compare also Borgardt , Brookhart , Burgert , Burghart , Burkart , and Burkett .
Germanized form of Polish and Czech Burkert , Czech Purkert or Purkart, and Slovenian Purkart: from a German variant and Slavicized forms of the medieval German personal name Burkhard (see 1 above).

He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. nameMeanings.name: Malsen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Malin   and   Mayland  .
Irish: variant of   Mallon  .
Spanish (Mallén): habitational name from Mallén in Zaragoza province.

variants.name: Malin
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Malin, a diminutive of Mall or Mald (Maud). See Mall and compare Mallinson and Maude 2.

Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metronymic from the Yiddish female personal name Male (a back-formation from Malka as if it contained the Slavic diminutive suffix -ke), with the addition of the Slavic possessive suffix -in.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): habitational name from Malin, a place in Ukraine.

nameMeanings.name: Hendrik
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Henrik .

variants.name: Henry
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  ♂ ‘Wondrous’ or ‘enlightenment’.
2  ♀ ‘Fine jade’.

en.lifeSummary: When Hendrik II van Cuyck van Malsen was born about 1140, in Betuwe, Gelderland, Netherlands, his father, Herman II van Cuijk, was 38 and his mother, Alvaradis De Chiny, was 31. He married Sophia Rhenen Lady of Herpen about 1160. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died in 1204, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hendrik II van Cuyck van Malsen est né environ 1140, à Betuwe, Gueldre, Pays-Bas, son père, Herman II van Cuijk, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Alvaradis De Chiny, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Sophia Rhenen Lady of Herpen environ 1160. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en 1204, à l’âge de 65 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hendrik II van Cuyck van Malsen um 1140 in Betuwe, Geldern, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Herman II van Cuijk 38 und seine Mutter Alvaradis De Chiny 31. Er hat um 1160 Sophia Rhenen Lady of Herpen geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Er starb 1204 im Alter von 65 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hendrik II van Cuyck van Malsen è nato il circa 1140 nel Betuwe, Gelderland, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Herman II van Cuijk, aveva 38 anni e il suo madre, Alvaradis De Chiny, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Sophia Rhenen Lady of Herpen circa 1160. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1204, all'età di 65 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Hendrik II van Cuyck van Malsenが約1140年にオランダジェルダーランドベトゥウで生まれた時，父Herman II van Cuijkは38歳，母Alvaradis De Chinyは31歳であった。約1160年にSophia Rhenen Lady of Herpenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1204年に65歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hendrik II van Cuyck van Malsen(이)가 대략 1140년 네덜란드 글델랜드 베투베에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Herman II van Cuijk(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Alvaradis De Chiny(은)는 31세였다. 그는 대략 1160년 Sophia Rhenen Lady of Herpen(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1204년 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt., Cuijk, Noord-Brabant, Nederland: Cuijk, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos
pt.Cuijk, North Brabant, Netherlands: Cuijk, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hendrik II van Cuyck van Malsen nasceu aproximadamente 1140, em Betuwe, Gheldria, Países Baixos, seu pai, Herman II van Cuijk, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Alvaradis De Chiny, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Sophia Rhenen Lady of Herpen aproximadamente 1160. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1204, com 65 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hendrik II van Cuyck van Malsen родился около 1140 г. в местности бетув, джелдирланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Herman II van Cuijk было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Alvaradis De Chiny, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Sophia Rhenen Lady of Herpen около 1160 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер в 1204 г. в возрасте 65 лет.

es., Cuijk, Noord-Brabant, Nederland: Cuijk, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos
es.Cuijk, North Brabant, Netherlands: Cuijk, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hendrik II van Cuyck van Malsen nació aproximadamente 1140, en Betuwe, Gelderland, Países Bajos, su papá, Herman II van Cuijk, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Alvaradis De Chiny, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Sophia Rhenen Lady of Herpen aproximadamente 1160. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió en 1204, a la edad de 65 años.
